title: Apple and Google Agree to App Store Changes After U.K. Scrutiny
description: Apple and Alphabet have agreed to implement changes to their app stores to enhance transparency and competition, following scrutiny from the U.K.'s Competition and Markets Authority (CMA). The CMA stated that these commitments will ensure fairer app reviews and provide developers with greater access to tools for creating competing products. This decision addresses concerns over the dominant control exerted by Apple's App Store and Google's Play Store within the mobile ecosystem.

title: 5 Blue-Chip Stocks to Buy as Dow Hits a New Milestone
description: The Dow Index recently crossed the 50,000 mark for the first time, signaling a shift in market participant interest from technology to cyclical stocks. This article recommends five blue-chip stocks with a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) that are poised for continued growth. These include Apple Inc., NVIDIA Corp., The Goldman Sachs Group Inc., Salesforce Inc., and Walmart Inc., each highlighted for their strong performance, strategic initiatives, and favorable growth prospects.

title: The Glass Architecture of AI: A Comprehensive Research Feature on Corning Inc. (GLW)
description: Corning Inc. (GLW) has transformed from a traditional glass and ceramics manufacturer into a critical enabler of the generative AI revolution, leveraging its fiber-optic solutions and glass substrates for next-generation semiconductors. The company's strategic "Springboard" growth plan, coupled with significant partnerships with tech giants like Meta and Apple, has led to substantial financial growth and a re-rating by the market. Despite risks such as valuation and geopolitical exposure, Corning is well-positioned to benefit from the increasing demand for high-bandwidth connectivity and advanced chip packaging, supported by U.S. industrial policy.

title: Apple Inc. (AAPL) Bernstein Raises Valuation Forecast
description: Bernstein analyst Toni Sacconaghi has increased the 12-month price target for Apple Inc. (AAPL) to $340.00 from $325.00, maintaining an "Outperform" rating. This upward revision is due to improved visibility into Apple's high-margin and consistent services growth, along with its strong capital return strategy. Despite potential near-term share price volatility and mixed hardware demand, Bernstein believes Apple's ecosystem strength, services mix, and disciplined capital allocation position it for market outperformance.

title: The making of MX Master 4: How Logitech designed a premium wireless mouse with haptic vibrations
description: Logitech's VP Anatoliy Polyanker discussed the design of the MX Master 4, a premium wireless mouse featuring haptic feedback. Inspired by smartphones and PCs, the mouse aims to provide an immersive, multisensory experience for creative professionals, enhancing productivity through features like the Action Ring and seamless device transitions. The design prioritizes desirability, durability, and sustainability, with a focus on software-driven customization and integration of AI tools.

title: Tech Views and Takeaways From 4Q25 Earnings - Raymond James - Commentaries
description: Raymond James' Larry Adam provides an analysis of 4Q25 earnings, highlighting robust double-digit growth for the S&P 500 and strong performance from mega-cap tech (MAGMAN) despite recent tech stock sell-offs. The report emphasizes the accelerating impact of AI across various industries, driving significant capital expenditure increases and broadening market performance beyond just the tech sector. Furthermore, emerging markets are showing strong momentum, particularly in chip-heavy economies, driven by impressive earnings growth.

title: Qualcomm (QCOM) Beats Estimates as Piper Sandler Reiterates Overweight Rating
description: Qualcomm (QCOM) reported stronger-than-expected earnings and revenue for Q1 fiscal 2026, with EPS of $3.50 and revenue of $12.25 billion, beating analyst forecasts. Despite the beat, Piper Sandler reiterated an Overweight rating with a $200 price target, noting tempered March guidance due to memory constraints at OEMs, particularly in China, which will impact the handset business, though Automotive and IoT segments show strong growth. The firm anticipates a longer-term impact from memory conditions and the Apple agreement, with potential boosts from clarity on Qualcomm's data center strategy.

title: Ragnarok: Twilight Official Launching in Vietnam on February 10, 2026
description: GRAVITY Co., Ltd. has officially launched its MMORPG mobile game, Ragnarok: Twilight, in Vietnam on February 10, 2026. This launch follows successful performances in other regions, including China, Taiwan, Hong Kong, Macau, and various Southeast Asian countries where the game achieved high rankings in downloads and grossing charts. Gravity aims to continue its good service in Vietnam and encourages users to enjoy the game's features and celebratory events.

title: Top-ranked mobile RPG Ragnarok: Twilight debuts in Vietnam
description: GRAVITY (NasdaqGM: GRVY) has officially launched its MMORPG mobile game, Ragnarok: Twilight, in Vietnam on February 10, 2026. This launch follows the game's significant success in other Asian markets, where it achieved high rankings in both free downloads and top-grossing lists on Google Play and Apple App Store. The game offers features like a hero transformation system, 100% rare-equipment/card MVP drops, and solo-friendly exclusive MVP dungeons.

title: Apple and Google agree app store changes to appease UK regulator
description: Apple and Google have agreed to implement changes to their app stores to address concerns raised by the UK's antitrust regulator, the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA). These changes aim to make app stores fairer and more transparent for developers. Both companies stated their continued commitment to user experience and developer opportunities while collaborating with the CMA.

title: Bull of the Day: Broadcom (AVGO)
description: Broadcom (AVGO) is highlighted as a "Strong Buy" due to its dominant positions in the semiconductor and software industries, particularly benefiting from the AI data center buildout. The company offers an alternative to Nvidia with its custom AI accelerators and has high-margin recurring revenue from its software segment, including VMware. Broadcom boasts a robust growth outlook, consistently beating earnings estimates, and strong technical performance.

title: Jim Cramer on Apple (AAPL): “Doesn’t Hurt That They’ve Avoided Spending Hundreds of Billions of Dollars on Data Centers”
description: Jim Cramer highlighted Apple's strong financial quarter, marked by excellent iPhone sales and robust performance in China, which was previously a weaker market for the company. He noted that Apple's strategy of partnering with Google's Gemini on AI has allowed it to avoid massive expenditures on data centers, contributing to its financial health. Cramer reiterated his long-standing advice to "own Apple, don’t trade it," emphasizing its investment stability.

title: Jim Cramer on Apple (AAPL): "Doesn't Hurt That They've Avoided Spending Hundreds of Billions of Dollars on Data Centers"
description: Jim Cramer recently discussed Apple's strong financial performance, particularly its blowout iPhone sales and robust numbers from China. He highlighted Apple's strategic advantage in avoiding massive expenditures on data centers by partnering with Google's Gemini for AI, a move Cramer believes has contributed to its recent stock rally. Cramer advises investors to "own Apple, don't trade it."

title: Stifel initiates coverage on CEVA stock with Buy rating, $30 target By Investing.com
description: Stifel has initiated coverage on CEVA Inc. (NASDAQ:CEVA) with a Buy rating and a $30 price target, citing the company's strong position in wireless connectivity IP for power-efficient on-device sensing and inference applications. The firm believes CEVA's low power NPU and DSP IP portfolios are well-placed to benefit from AI infrastructure growth towards the network edge, despite recognizing near-term challenges such as mobile handset demand and uncertainty regarding Apple's modem efforts. CEVA's high gross profit margins (87.17%) and recent strategic partnerships, including a NPU deal with Microchip Technology, validate its long-term strategy in the edge-AI market.

title: Apple Inc. $AAPL Holdings Decreased by TLW Wealth Management LLC
description: TLW Wealth Management LLC reduced its stake in Apple Inc. ($AAPL) by 6.5% during the third quarter, reducing its holdings to 50,473 shares valued at $12.85 million. Despite this, Apple remains the firm's sixth-largest holding. Meanwhile, other institutional investors like Vanguard Group Inc., State Street Corp, Geode Capital Management LLC, Norges Bank, and Northern Trust Corp significantly increased their positions in Apple.

title: Montis Financial LLC Has $19.84 Million Stake in Apple Inc. $AAPL
description: Montis Financial LLC increased its stake in Apple Inc. by 15.8% to 77,919 shares, valued at $19.84 million, making Apple its seventh-largest holding. This comes as Apple surpassed quarterly expectations with robust EPS and revenue figures, revenue growth, and a declared dividend. Analyst sentiment toward Apple remains largely positive, with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and upward adjustments to price targets from several brokerages.

title: EdgeRock Capital LLC Sells 4,794 Shares of Apple Inc. $AAPL
description: EdgeRock Capital LLC reduced its stake in Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) by 15.1% in the third quarter, selling 4,794 shares and now owning 26,971 shares valued at $6.87 million. Despite this sale, Apple remains the 17th largest holding for EdgeRock Capital LLC, making up 1.9% of its portfolio. Other institutional investors have varied their positions, while analyst sentiment towards Apple remains "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $291.70.

title: Davis Rea LTD. Sells 40,173 Shares of Apple Inc. $AAPL
description: Davis Rea LTD. has significantly reduced its stake in Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) by selling 40,173 shares, decreasing its position by 64.9%. Despite this, Apple remains a major holding for the fund, making up 1.3% of its portfolio. The article also details other institutional investor activity, analyst ratings, and recent financial performance of Apple.

title: TSMC Stock Is on a Roll. Rising Sales and Trump Tariff News Could Keep It Going.
description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) reported a 20% increase in January revenue from the previous quarter, signaling its strong position in the semiconductor industry. This growth, coupled with potential U.S. tariff relief, could further boost the company's performance. The article suggests that TSMC's stock momentum is likely to continue.

title: Apple Inc. $AAPL is Ritholtz Wealth Management's 6th Largest Position
description: Ritholtz Wealth Management increased its stake in Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) by 4.7% during the third quarter, making it their 6th largest position and accounting for 2.8% of their holdings. This comes as Apple faces neutral to positive sentiment from analysts, with upcoming product cycles and AI integration initiatives balancing out concerns about competition from pure-play AI companies. The company recently reported strong earnings, exceeding analyst estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend.

title: Keysight Technologies stock hits all-time high at 233.16 USD By Investing.com
description: Keysight Technologies (KEYS) stock reached an all-time high of $233.16 USD, reflecting strong investor confidence and market performance with a 32.49% increase over the past year. The company maintains a robust financial position and has recently unveiled new electric vehicle charging test solutions and partnerships, including collaborations with Point2 Technology for AI data centers and Airbus UpNext for 5G non-terrestrial networks. These advancements highlight Keysight's strategic initiatives and operational excellence across various technological sectors.

title: Taiwan Rejects U.S. Demand for 40% Chip Supply Chain Shift
description: Taiwan has rejected a U.S. proposal to relocate 40% of its semiconductor supply chain, deeming it "impossible." This decision highlights Taiwan's intent to maintain its strategic chip dominance and the geopolitical tensions surrounding the global tech hardware ecosystem. Major U.S. tech companies like Apple, Nvidia, and AMD rely heavily on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) for advanced chip production, making any significant disruption problematic.

title: Apple Just Won 2026? The Brutal Truth About Apple Inc. And Its Stock
description: The article discusses whether Apple Inc. stock is a worthwhile investment, given its strong brand power, ecosystem lock-in, and growing services revenue. It differentiates between Apple's image as a consumer tech status symbol and its financial performance as a "must-have" core holding for long-term investors. The piece advises a cautious approach, distinguishing between hype and fundamental business strength.

title: Ferrari Officially Announces Luce EV with Teaser Images
description: Ferrari has officially announced its first all-electric vehicle, the Luce, revealing interior details and specifications. The four-door, four-seat GT will feature over 1,000 horsepower, a 122 kWh battery, and an interior co-designed with LoveFrom, emphasizing physical controls over touchscreens. The full exterior reveal is scheduled for May 2026, with an expected price upwards of €500,000.

title: Apple Stock Price Today: Market Trends and Investor Focus
description: Apple Inc.'s stock (AAPL) is currently trading between $272 and $278 per share, with market attention drawn to its services and brand strategy. The article highlights Apple Music's role in the Super Bowl LX halftime show as a recent driver of sentiment, alongside strong hardware sales and a growing services ecosystem. With a market capitalization over $4 trillion, analysts largely maintain "buy" ratings, reinforcing confidence in the company's long-term outlook.

title: GM hires new head of strategy from Lucid Motors
description: General Motors has appointed Claudia Gast, formerly of Lucid Motors, as its deputy CFO and VP of strategy, corporate development, and technology partnerships. She will begin on March 1 and will focus on scouting partnerships with technology companies, reporting to CEO Mary Barra and CFO Paul Jacobson. Gast replaces former Tesla executive Zach Kirkman, who is leaving GM for other opportunities.

title: Software Poses ‘All-Time’ Risk to Speculative Credit, Deutsche Bank Warns
description: Deutsche Bank analysts have warned that the software and technology sectors represent one of the greatest concentration risks ever seen in the speculative-grade credit market. These sectors together account for a substantial portion of speculative debt, including high-yield debt, leveraged loans, and US private credit. The analysts, led by Steve Caprio, highlighted the significant financial figures involved, with software comprising $597 billion and technology $681 billion of this credit universe.

title: Consumers are receiving payments from the Lopez vs. Apple settlement, which is not a scam
description: The article confirms that payments from the Lopez vs. Apple settlement are legitimate, dispelling fears that they might be a scam. This class-action lawsuit against Apple involved allegations that its Siri voice assistant illegally obtained and sold consumers' confidential communications, leading to a $95 million settlement. Recipients have been getting payments via email, direct deposit, or paper checks, with individual payments capped at $20 per Apple device.

title: Sandisk and Micron slip as Samsung rushes new product into production
description: Sandisk and Micron shares are falling after reports that Samsung will begin mass production of its HBM4 memory product earlier than expected. While Micron already produces HBM, Sandisk is developing a high-bandwidth flash NAND memory product for AI data centers. This move by Samsung indicates that the high prices for memory chips, which have recently boosted profits for companies like Sandisk, may not continue indefinitely as supply increases.

title: Abrdn Total Dynamic Dividend Fund stock hits 52-week high at 10.18 USD
description: The Abrdn Total Dynamic Dividend Fund (AOD)

title: Apple class action lawsuit over Siri BIPA violations certified
description: An Illinois state judge has certified a class action lawsuit against Apple Inc. concerning its Siri feature, alleging violations of the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA). The lawsuit claims Apple failed to obtain informed consent before collecting, storing, and using voiceprints from Siri users. This ruling allows approximately 3 million Apple users in Illinois to proceed collectively with the legal challenge.

title: Brighthouse Financial stock hits 52-week high at 12.08 USD
description: Brighthouse Financial Inc. (BHFAM) stock reached a 52-week high of $12.08, despite a significant 19.47% decrease over the past year. The company's stock currently offers a 9.57% dividend yield and trades at a P/E ratio of 4.44. InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is undervalued, boasting strong fundamentals, including a healthy 1.55 current ratio.

title: Are Wall Street Analysts Bullish on Goldman Sachs Stock?
description: Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. (GS) has demonstrated strong financial performance, outperforming the S&P 500 and iShares U.S. Broker-Dealers & Securities Exchanges ETF over the past year and year-to-date. Its success is attributed to robust investment banking, significant asset and wealth management inflows, and strategic focus. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus, with a mean price target suggesting a slight premium to current levels and a high target indicating significant upside potential.

title: Are Wall Street Analysts Bullish on Goldman Sachs Stock?
description: Goldman Sachs (GS) has demonstrated strong performance, outperforming the S&P 500 and the iShares U.S. Broker-Dealers & Securities Exchanges ETF over the past year and year-to-date. This success is attributed to robust investment banking, significant asset and wealth management inflows, and a refined strategic focus. Analysts generally hold a "Moderate Buy" consensus on GS stock, anticipating continued growth in EPS for the current fiscal year.

title: TCS, Infosys, HCL Tech, Wipro: Lessons for IT investors from AI’s iPhone
description: The article discusses the significant disruption caused by AI in the software industry, drawing parallels to how the iPhone transformed the mobile phone industry in 2007. It highlights how incumbents like Nokia and BlackBerry failed to adapt, leading to their downfall, and warns Indian IT services investors (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL Tech) that their current high valuations might not account for the profound disruption AI could bring, advising caution over a "buy the dip" mentality.

title: Micron Stock Can’t Stop Going Up and Goldman Sachs Knows Exactly Why
description: The technology sector is experiencing a significant shortage of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips, largely due to the high demand from AI data centers. This "great memory crunch," identified by Goldman Sachs, is driving up prices and reducing production for device manufacturers. However, it's greatly benefiting HBM producers like Micron Technology, whose stock has surged due to its crucial role in supplying HBM for AI and other electronics.

title: Thiel Bypasses Palantir and Nvidia for Meta, Tesla, Apple in Contrarian AI Play
description: Peter Thiel's hedge fund is focusing its AI investments on companies that use artificial intelligence for applications rather than those that build the infrastructure. His fund holds significant positions in Meta Platforms, Tesla, and Apple, while notably avoiding Palantir (which he co-founded) and Nvidia. This strategy highlights a contrarian bet on AI application plays, with Apple now making up a substantial portion of his portfolio after liquidating Nvidia holdings.

title: Goldman Named The Issue That’s Pushing SanDisk Stock to the Moon
description: SanDisk Corp. (NASDAQ: SNDK) stock has seen an extraordinary 1,561% increase in 12 months, driven by the severe global shortage of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips crucial for AI infrastructure. Goldman Sachs identified this "great memory crunch" as a significant factor, with HBM supply not expected to ease until 2028 at the earliest. SanDisk's Q2 revenues hit $3 billion (up 61% YoY) and earnings surged to $6.20 per share (a 5x increase), forecasting continued growth due to insatiable AI demand.

title: Campbell & Co Slashes Apple Stake by More Than 70%
description: Campbell & Co Investment Adviser LLC significantly reduced its stake in Apple Inc. by over 70% in the third quarter, selling 11,529 shares. Despite this divestment, Apple remains a strong performer with increasing revenue and a growing installed base, indicating that Campbell & Co's move is likely part of a broader portfolio rebalancing rather than a bearish outlook on Apple specifically. The article also notes Apple's upcoming ex-dividend date of February 9, 2026, for a $0.26 per share payout.

title: Thrivent Financial for Lutherans Has $3.64 Million Stock Holdings in Xylem Inc. $XYL
description: Thrivent Financial for Lutherans significantly reduced its stake in Xylem Inc. (NYSE:XYL) by 48.3% during the third quarter, now holding 24,650 shares valued at $3.64 million. Despite this reduction, institutional investors collectively own 87.96% of Xylem's stock, with major new positions opened by Norges Bank and ABN AMRO. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus for XYL, with an average price target of $165.80, against its current trading price near $142.

title: Apple Inc. $AAPL Position Lowered by Gerber LLC
description: Gerber LLC significantly reduced its stake in Apple Inc. by 37.2% in the third quarter, decreasing its holdings to 21,825 shares valued at $5.56 million, now representing 3.5% of its portfolio. Despite this, Apple reported strong Q1 earnings, beating estimates with $2.84 EPS and $143.76 billion in revenue, and declared a $0.26 quarterly dividend. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $291.70, viewing strategic moves like allowing third-party AI in CarPlay as potential growth drivers.

title: Apple Inc. $AAPL is Blue Square Asset Management LLC's 8th Largest Position
description: Blue Square Asset Management LLC has increased its stake in Apple Inc. (AAPL), making it their 8th largest holding. The article details Apple's strong Q3 earnings, positive sentiment surrounding its AI integrations in CarPlay, and continued institutional interest, while also acknowledging potential headwinds like scaled-back AI health projects and insider selling. Analyst ratings maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus for the stock.

title: Stocks Showing Rising Market Leadership: GlobalFoundries Earns 81 RS Rating
description: GlobalFoundries (GFS) has seen its Relative Strength (RS) Rating increase from 77 to 81, indicating strong price performance. This proprietary rating from Investor's Business Daily assesses a stock's price leadership on a scale from 1 to 99, with higher scores denoting better performance. The chipmaker has recently shown significant market leadership with its RS Rating reaching the 80-plus level multiple times, even as it expands into AI chipmaking.

title: Tech Hits a Wall—Energy Breaks Out
description: As tech stocks falter and the broader market corrects, energy stocks have surged, indicating a significant sector rotation. The article highlights that traditional energy companies have been top performers this year, with the iShares Global Energy ETF up almost 15%, while tech remains flat. This shift suggests that investors should focus on sectors demonstrating clear strength, particularly energy.

title: Apple (AAPL) Should Be Up Again, Says Jim Cramer
description: Jim Cramer remains optimistic about Apple (AAPL), advising investors to own rather than trade the stock. Goldman Sachs and Bernstein both reiterated "Buy" and "Outperform" ratings respectively, citing improving App Store trends and strong iPhone revenue. Cramer believes Apple is well-positioned for further growth, especially in the context of its memory industry navigation and increasing market share in China.

title: Qualcomm's (QCOM) Hurt By Memory, Says Jim Cramer
description: Jim Cramer commented on Qualcomm (QCOM) following its earnings report, stating that the company is "hurt by memory." This comes as Cantor Fitzgerald suggested Qualcomm might issue weaker-than-expected guidance due to concerns about Apple's products and challenges in the Chinese smartphone market. Despite acknowledging QCOM's investment potential, the article highlights a focus on AI stocks for higher returns and limited downside risk.

title: Qualcomm’s (QCOM) Hurt By Memory, Says Jim Cramer
description: Jim Cramer commented on Qualcomm's recent earnings report, suggesting that the company is being hurt by a lack of memory, comparing it to Sony. Qualcomm's shares have fallen significantly, and analysts anticipate weaker-than-expected guidance due to concerns about Apple's products and challenges in the Chinese smartphone market. Cramer noted that Apple, despite some complaints, is performing well due to its memory capabilities.

title: Apple (AAPL) Should Be Up Again, Says Jim Cramer
description: Jim Cramer continues to recommend holding Apple (AAPL) shares, believing the company should see further gains. Major investment banks, Goldman Sachs and Bernstein, have reiterated "Buy" and "Outperform" ratings, respectively, citing strong App Store performance, record iPhone revenue, and effective navigation of the memory industry. Cramer highlights Apple's potential for a lower-cost phone and increasing market share in China as factors for its continued upward trajectory.

title: Alibaba stock ends skid after Friday jump as Qwen giveaway hits glitches and earnings near
description: Alibaba's U.S.-listed shares climbed roughly 3% on Friday, recovering after a volatile period, partly due to investor re-entry into tech stocks. The company's AI ambitions gained attention as its Qwen app experienced a surge in users following a 3 billion yuan Lunar New Year giveaway, briefly knocking the app offline and facing restrictions on WeChat for sharing campaign links. Despite these glitches and privacy concerns, Qwen rapidly rose in the App Store, and Alibaba is now looking ahead to its quarterly earnings release on February 19th, with investors particularly interested in the sustained momentum of the Qwen rollout and spending patterns.

title: Apple Prepares to Enable Third-Party AI Voice Assistants in CarPlay
description: Apple is expected to integrate third-party AI voice assistants like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini into CarPlay within the coming months. This move will allow users to access these advanced chatbots through dedicated apps, expanding CarPlay's functionality beyond current categories. While enhancing user options for conversational AI, Siri will maintain its primary role as the default voice assistant for core vehicle and iPhone controls.

title: Buy 4 Cloud Computing Stocks as Digital Transformation Gains Traction
description: This article highlights the increasing adoption of cloud computing due to its flexibility, scalability, and cost-effectiveness, driving digital transformation across various industries. It recommends four key cloud computing stocks: Alphabet (GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), IBM (IBM), and Arista Networks (ANET), detailing their strengths and contributions to the cloud market. The global cloud computing market is projected to reach $3,349.6 billion by 2033, underscoring significant investment opportunities.

title: Buy 4 Cloud Computing Stocks as Digital Transformation Gains Traction
description: The article highlights the growing importance of cloud computing in digital transformation and identifies four key stocks for investors: Alphabet (GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), IBM (IBM), and Arista Networks (ANET). It details how cloud computing offers cost savings, flexibility, and enhanced productivity for businesses across various sectors, with the global market projected to reach $3,349.6 billion by 2033. The piece provides an overview of each company's cloud offerings and market position, making a case for their inclusion in investment portfolios.

title: Jim Cramer on Microsoft (MSFT): “I Think They Need to Explain Their Story Better”
description: Jim Cramer commented on Microsoft's recent stock decline, attributing it to decelerating revenue growth in its Azure cloud unit and what he perceives as low demand for its Copilot AI platform. He noted that Microsoft's continued spending on AI infrastructure and the market's perception of OpenAI as a less reliable customer contributed to the stock's nearly 10% fall. Cramer believes Microsoft needs to better explain its narrative, though he finds the stock more enticing at 24 times earnings.

title: Why Traditional Security Fails in the Age of Non-Deterministic AI
description: Traditional cybersecurity, built on deterministic models, is failing to protect against the non-deterministic nature of AI applications. AI's adaptive and evolving models create a constantly changing threat surface, rendering rule-based security tools ineffective and leading to a fragmented security posture with the adoption of numerous point tools. A unified platform approach, like Prisma AIRS, is necessary to discover, assess, and protect the entire AI ecosystem in real-time, enabling secure and rapid innovation.

title: Innovation at AI Speed: Why Security is the Bedrock of Brand Trust
description: The article emphasizes that security is crucial for building brand trust and enabling rapid innovation in the age of AI. It argues that while AI dramatically increases the speed of innovation, a secure foundation is essential to prevent this speed from becoming a liability. The author, Kelly Waldher, highlights how integrating security guardrails allows companies to scale oversight, maintain brand safety, and gain a competitive edge in the AI market, as demonstrated by Palo Alto Networks' "Be a Genius" campaign.

title: Howmet Aerospace stock hits all-time high at 226.99 USD
description: Howmet Aerospace (HWM) stock reached an all-time high of $226.99, closing at $227.49, surpassing its previous 52-week high. The company has shown a 76.06% return over the past year, holds a robust Piotroski Score of 9, and has a market capitalization of $90.5 billion. Recent strategic moves include announcing a quarterly dividend and the acquisition of Consolidated Aerospace Manufacturing for $1.8 billion, with analysts maintaining positive ratings and raising price targets.

title: Goldman’s Solomon says AI-driven software selloff overdone
description: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon stated that the recent selloff in software stocks due to AI competition concerns was an overreaction, noting that many companies will adapt successfully. He also expressed optimism about the U.S. economic outlook for the year, citing strong fiscal stimulus and deregulation as growth drivers, while acknowledging ongoing concerns about trade, inflation, and geopolitical tensions.

title: Most big companies race to revamp virtualization for AI, HPE finds
description: A new HPE survey reveals that over two-thirds of enterprises plan significant changes to their virtualization strategies within the next two years, primarily driven by the demands of AI, cost unpredictability, and performance needs rather than just licensing costs. Only 5% of companies are fully prepared for this "Great Virtualization Reset." The shift is towards flexible, hybrid operating models, with a strong emphasis on unified backup, cyber-recovery, cross-platform governance, and integrated AIOps as essential components for modern virtualization to support AI workloads.

title: GE Vernova Inc. $GEV Shares Purchased by Wealthfront Advisers LLC
description: Wealthfront Advisers LLC increased its holdings in GE Vernova Inc. (NYSE:GEV) by 4.1% in the third quarter, bringing its total to 34,468 shares valued at $21.19 million. Other institutional investors like Vanguard, State Street, and Invesco also significantly increased their stakes. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $788.24, following GE Vernova's strong Q4 earnings beat and increased dividend payout.

title: Security Analytics Market Growing at 17.1% CAGR, Driven
description: The Security Analytics Market is projected to grow at a 17.1% CAGR from 2024 to 2031, driven by increasing cyber threats and the adoption of AI/ML, cloud-native analytics, and solutions from key players like IBM, Splunk, and Microsoft. North America currently leads the market, while Asia-Pacific is the fastest-growing region. Recent developments highlight the convergence of AI/ML with SIEM/XDR, cloud-native solutions, and strategic acquisitions to enhance threat detection and response capabilities.

title: Gartner: European spending on sovereign cloud IaaS to nearly double in 2026
description: European organizations are projected to nearly double their spending on sovereign cloud Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) in 2026, reaching $12.6 billion, due to geopolitical tensions and a desire to reduce reliance on US hyperscalers. This trend, termed "geopatriation," will see businesses moving existing workloads to local cloud providers, posing a challenge for hyperscalers who must adapt to avoid losing market share. While US-owned "sovereign" cloud services exist, their true sovereignty is questioned, suggesting partnerships with local providers might be a better approach for hyperscalers.

title: Incyte (NASDAQ:INCY) Surprises With Q4 CY2025 Sales
description: Incyte Corporation (NASDAQ: INCY) reported Q4 CY2025 revenue of $1.51 billion, significantly exceeding Wall Street's estimates by 11.4% and representing a 27.8% year-on-year growth. Despite the strong revenue performance, the company's non-GAAP profit of $1.80 per share missed analyst consensus estimates by 6.1%. Shares traded down 3.8% immediately after the report, indicating that investors were hoping for even stronger results, particularly concerning profitability.

title: ProShare Advisors LLC Has $85.21 Million Stake in Marvell Technology, Inc. $MRVL
description: ProShare Advisors LLC reduced its stake in Marvell Technology by 20.6% in Q3, now holding 1,013,545 shares valued at $85.21 million. Marvell Technology reported strong Q3 earnings, beating analyst estimates with $0.76 EPS and $2.07 billion in revenue, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a $115.31 target price. The company also recently paid a quarterly dividend of $0.06 per share and saw an EVP sell 5,000 shares.

title: Oppenheimer Asset Management Inc. Trims Stake in Parker-Hannifin Corporation $PH
description: Oppenheimer Asset Management Inc. reduced its stake in Parker-Hannifin Corporation by 4.9% in the third quarter of 2025, selling 1,045 shares and retaining 20,132 shares valued at approximately $15.26 million. Despite insider selling activity totaling $9.65 million over the past 90 days, institutional investors hold 82.44% of the stock. Parker-Hannifin surpassed Q3 earnings expectations and announced a quarterly dividend of $1.80, maintaining a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts with a target price around $990.

title: DA Davidson Thinks Amazon.com (AMZN) is "Losing the Lead" - Here's Why
description: DA Davidson downgraded Amazon.com (AMZN) from Buy to Neutral and lowered its price target to $175, citing concerns that the company is losing its lead in cloud computing and lagging in the AI-driven retail landscape. The firm noted AWS's slower growth compared to competitors like Azure and Google Cloud, and suggested Amazon might need significant investment in AI to remain competitive. Despite a separate price target revision from Bank of America maintaining a Buy rating, DA Davidson believes Amazon faces strategic disadvantages without direct AI integrations.

title: Monday.com Q4 and FY2025 Earnings: Revenue Tops $1.2 Billion as AI Products Take Centre Stage
description: Monday.com reported strong Q4 and FY2025 earnings with revenue topping $1.2 billion, driven by enterprise growth and the rapid adoption of its AI products. However, the company's conservative FY2026 guidance, projecting 18-19% growth below Wall Street expectations, caused its stock to drop. Monday.com is focusing on its AI platform, including Sidekick, Vibe, Agents, and Workflows, to shift from work management to work execution, targeting labor leverage and scaling output without increasing headcount.

title: Hartline Investment Corp Sells 2,339 Shares of Microsoft Corporation $MSFT
description: Hartline Investment Corp recently decreased its stake in Microsoft Corporation by 3.4%, selling 2,339 shares and retaining 65,819 shares valued at $34.09 million. Microsoft remains Hartline's fifth-largest holding, constituting approximately 3.5% of its portfolio. The sale comes amidst mixed investor sentiment regarding Microsoft, with strong earnings and AI monetization prospects balanced by concerns over significant AI capital expenditures and some analyst downgrades despite a generally positive "Moderate Buy" rating from Wall Street.

title: Below-Expected Azure Growth Hurt Microsoft’s (MSFT) Performance in Q4
description: Sustainable Growth Advisers (SGA) reported that Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ:MSFT) was a detractor in Q4 2025 for its U.S. Large Cap Growth Strategy portfolio, despite a solid overall quarterly report. This was primarily due to Azure growth coming in slightly below expectations at 39% and weaker-than-expected guidance, attributed to supply constraints. While SGA acknowledges challenges like elevated capital expenditures and lackluster adoption of Copilot, it maintains an above-average weight position in Microsoft, confident in its long-term growth potential through strong pricing power, high switching costs, and leadership in cloud and AI.

title: New Buy Rating for Palo Alto Networks (PANW), the Technology Giant
description: Barclays analyst Saket Kalia has maintained a Buy rating on Palo Alto Networks (PANW) with a $200 price target. This rating comes despite mixed sentiment from other firms, including a recent downgrade from TipRanks – Google to a Hold. Palo Alto Networks reported quarterly revenue of $2.47 billion and a net profit of $334 million, but corporate insider sentiment is negative due to increased selling activity.

title: Occidental Asset Management LLC Has $29.25 Million Holdings in Microsoft Corporation $MSFT
description: Occidental Asset Management LLC increased its stake in Microsoft Corporation by 7.7% in the third quarter, bringing their total holdings to 56,474 shares valued at $29.25 million. Microsoft represents 3.7% of their investment portfolio. Other institutional investors like Norges Bank, Nuveen LLC, and Vanguard Group Inc. also significantly altered their positions in Microsoft.

title: Southwest Gas stock hits 52-week high at 83.26 USD By Investing.com
description: Southwest Gas Holdings Inc. (SWX) has reached a 52-week high of $83.26, reflecting a 9.01% increase over the past year and strong investor confidence. This performance is supported by recent announcements including a quarterly cash dividend of $0.62 per share, the appointment of Justin S. Forsberg as CFO, and an increased price target of $97.00 from Jefferies. These developments highlight the company's resilience and growth potential in the energy sector amidst market fluctuations.

title: Microsoft and Oracle may be bargain stocks, according to this analysis
description: MarketWatch analysis suggests that Microsoft and Oracle stocks might be undervalued bargains. Both companies are projected to have significantly higher revenue growth rates than the S&P 500, yet their shares are trading at attractive price/earnings multiples following recent pullbacks. This situation creates a buying opportunity for investors in the software sector.

title: Oracle adds more than 40 agents to Fusion Cloud suite
description: Oracle has introduced over 40 new prebuilt artificial intelligence agents into its Fusion Cloud Applications suite, covering marketing, sales, service, and supply chain functions. These agents, running on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, are designed to automate and transform business processes, providing insights and taking actions with minimal human intervention. Customers can also use Oracle AI Agent Studio to create custom agents.

title: IBM Introduces Autonomous Storage with New FlashSystem Portfolio Powered by Agentic AI
description: IBM has launched the next generation of its FlashSystem portfolio, featuring agentic AI to usher in an era of autonomous storage. These new systems, including FlashSystem 5600, 7600, and 9600, significantly reduce manual storage management efforts by up to 90% and offer up to 40% greater data efficiency. A new fifth-generation FlashCore Module is engineered for real-time ransomware detection within one minute, enhanced resiliency, and data management, transforming storage into an intelligent, always-on layer.

title: Melius Downgrades Microsoft (MSFT) to Hold, Flags AI Capex and Cash Flow Risks
description: Melius analyst Ben Reitzes downgraded Microsoft (MSFT) from Buy to Hold with a $430 price target, citing concerns about AI-related capital expenditure and cash flow risks. The analyst believes Microsoft's 365 business faces threats from AI, requiring increased spending to compete with Amazon and Google, which could further impact free cash flow. Additionally, Melius noted the slow progress of Microsoft's AI assistant, Copilot, suggesting it may struggle against competitors and affect growth and margins in the Productivity segment, despite valuation concerns related to revised free cash flow estimates.

title: Microsoft Stock Rises 3% Buts Trades Cheaper Than IBM
description: For the first time in over a decade, Microsoft (MSFT) is trading at a lower forward price-to-earnings ratio than IBM (IBM), indicating a significant shift in how investors view the AI market. While Microsoft's substantial capital expenditure for AI infrastructure has led to a valuation compression, IBM's successful pivot to a software-centric and consulting-driven model has boosted its market credibility. This inversion highlights a broader market evolution where investors are increasingly prioritizing tangible ROI and capital discipline over pure enthusiasm in the AI sector.

title: Century Aluminum Kentucky smelter site to host data center
description: Century Aluminum has sold its former Hawesville, Kentucky smelter site to TeraWulf Inc., which plans to redevelop it into a data center for high-performance computing and AI workloads. Century will retain a non-controlling minority equity stake in the project. This move follows other significant investments by Century Aluminum in the US, including expansions and new smelter constructions, emphasizing its commitment to domestic primary aluminum production.

title: HubSpot (HUBS) Q4 Earnings: What To Expect
description: HubSpot (HUBS) is expected to report its Q4 earnings on Wednesday, with analysts forecasting an 18% year-on-year revenue growth to $829.9 million and adjusted earnings of $2.99 per share. The company has a strong history of beating revenue estimates, and peer results from ZoomInfo and LiveRamp offer mixed insights into the sales and marketing software segment. Despite a general downturn in sales and marketing software stocks, HubSpot's average analyst price target remains significantly higher than its current share price.

title: Radware’s New Agentic AI Protection Platform Might Change The Case For Investing In Radware (RDWR)
description: Radware recently launched its Agentic AI Protection Solution, designed to provide real-time behavioral defenses against agent-specific AI risks. This new platform aims to strengthen Radware's position in AI security and address vulnerabilities across various AI platforms. While the new offering might boost Radware's relevance with large customers, its immediate effect on financial performance and its ability to justify the company's valuation remain key considerations for investors.

title: Cognex (CGNX) Reports Q4: Everything You Need To Know Ahead Of Earnings
description: Cognex (CGNX) is set to report its Q4 earnings after market hours, with analysts expecting a 4.2% year-on-year revenue growth to $239.4 million and adjusted earnings of $0.22 per share. The company has a history of beating revenue estimates and its stock is up 10.9% over the last month, outperforming the broader tech hardware & electronics segment. Investors will be watching for performance against analysts' reconfirmed estimates given the recent market volatility.

title: Freeport-McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) Stock Price Up 4.9% - Time to Buy?
description: Freeport-McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) saw its stock price jump 4.9% to $63.65 on Monday, following a quarterly earnings beat with EPS of $0.47 and revenue of $5.63 billion. Several analysts have raised their price targets for the copper and gold producer, contributing to a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from MarketBeat. Despite a recent dividend cut to $0.075 per quarter and some insider selling, institutional investors hold a significant 80.77% stake in the company.

title: Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) Shares Up 3.2% - What's Next?
description: Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) shares rose 3.2% to $414 on Monday, driven by bullish analyst commentary and AI-related catalysts such as disclosing paying Copilot customers and a key AI hire. The company reported strong quarterly earnings, beating expectations with $4.14 EPS and $81.27 billion in revenue, and announced a $0.91 quarterly dividend. Despite some analyst downgrades due to AI capital expenditure concerns, the consensus remains a "Moderate Buy" with an average target price of around $592.

title: Palo Alto Networks AI Partner Shift Highlights Platform Growth And Valuation Gap
description: Palo Alto Networks has revamped its NextWave Partner Program, shifting focus to AI-driven security outcomes and platformization to incentivize technical expertise and high-margin services over point product sales. This move aims to deepen its role in customer environments and accelerate growth through its partner ecosystem in the AI era. Investors should monitor the adaptation of partners to this new model, its impact on recurring platform revenue, and the company's financial metrics such as profit margins and P/E ratio.

title: Puerto Rico transforms education with Microsoft 365 Copilot
description: Puerto Rico's Department of Education has implemented Microsoft 365 Copilot to revolutionize its education system, serving over 230,000 students and 20,000 educators. This initiative addresses the need for personalized learning, streamlined administration, and enhanced security, especially post-pandemic. Copilot's AI capabilities are now used to tailor activities for diverse student needs, improve curriculum planning, and free up teachers' time for more impactful interactions, leading to early improvements in standardized test scores and a more innovation-friendly school culture.

title: James Abernethy Sells 1,000 Shares of Peoples Bancorp of North Carolina (NASDAQ:PEBK) Stock
description: Director James Abernethy sold 1,000 shares of Peoples Bancorp of North Carolina (NASDAQ:PEBK) on February 9, 2026, for a total of $38,340, reducing his stake by 1.36%. The company has a consensus "Buy" rating from analysts and recently announced an annual dividend of $0.17 per share, yielding 46.0%. PEBK was trading flat at $38.61 with a market capitalization of $210.81 million.

title: How Higher 2025 Revenue And Legal Leadership Change At Brown & Brown (BRO) Has Changed Its Investment Story
description: Brown & Brown (BRO) recently reported increased full-year 2025 revenue of US$5.90 billion and net income of US$1.05 billion, though earnings per share declined slightly. Concurrently, the company announced the passing of its Chief Legal Officer, Robert Mathis, and the appointment of Eileen Akerson as interim CLO. This dual development prompts an examination of how revenue growth and leadership transition influence the investment narrative for Brown & Brown, especially concerning its ability to rebuild EPS growth and improve return on equity amidst existing financial pressures.

title: Toyota's Secret Weapon: Inside Fluorite, the Automaker's Surprising Leap Into Open-Source Game Engine Development
description: Toyota Motor Corporation is developing Fluorite, an open-source game engine built using C++ and Vulkan, for real-time 3D simulation, automotive visualization, and software-defined vehicle development. This move signals Toyota's strategic investment in controlling its own rendering stack, attracting software talent, and positioning itself within the evolving automotive industry's focus on software. Fluorite has the potential to become a foundational tool for the broader automotive supply chain if it can overcome the significant challenges of game engine development and cultural integration within Toyota.

title: Freedom Holding Corp. Eyes U.S. Debt Market to Fund Telecom and Data Center Expansion
description: Freedom Holding Corp., led by Kazakhstani billionaire Timur Turlov, is considering entering the U.S. debt market to raise $300-500 million for expanding its telecom infrastructure and building data centers. This move aims to fund large-scale investment in digital infrastructure to provide cloud services to major tech companies, government institutions, and private businesses, shifting the group's strategy towards a more capital-intensive, infrastructure-based business model. Turlov emphasizes the importance of building long-term relationships with U.S. investors and the strategic integration into the global capital market, while also exploring international expansion for its Superapp.

title: Gartner Says Worldwide Sovereign Cloud IaaS Spending Will Total $80B in 2026
description: Gartner forecasts that worldwide sovereign cloud Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) spending will reach $80 billion in 2026, marking a 35.6% increase from 2025. This surge is driven by countries outside the U.S. and China seeking digital and technological independence, with governments and regulated industries being the primary purchasers. The increasing demand for "geopatriation" projects is expected to shift 20% of current workloads from global to local cloud providers, pressuring hyperscalers to address national sovereignty concerns.

title: Microsoft Stock Rises Amid Sector Rotation and Analyst Optimism | 2026 Market Update - News and Statistics
description: Microsoft stock increased by 3.1% after analysts suggested it was oversold following a recent sector sell-off. This rise is attributed to institutional investors returning to established companies, with Barclays noting slow corporate transitions that benefit incumbents. The stock had previously dropped 11.8% due to mixed quarterly results, and it remains down 12.5% for the year and 23.7% below its 52-week high.

title: Amdocs Recognized as the Current Company to Beat in the 2025 Gartner AI Vendor Race: Amdocs Is the Company to Beat for AI in CSP Business Support Systems
description: Amdocs has been recognized by Gartner as the "Company to Beat" for AI in CSP Business Support Systems in their 2025 AI Vendor Race report. Gartner highlighted Amdocs' transformation to an AI-native solution architecture and unified data platform as key reasons for their leading position. Amdocs expressed delight in this recognition, viewing it as validation of their long-term vision to build AI, GenAI, and agentic platforms for service providers.

title: 3 Reasons to Hold ServiceNow Stock Despite a 42% Decline in 3 Months
description: ServiceNow's stock has declined significantly, reflecting concerns about short-term growth and increased AI/cloud spending. However, the company is seeing rapid adoption of its AI-native products like Now Assist, benefiting from expanding partner alliances, and has an appealing valuation. Despite near-term pressures from AI spending and acquisition risks, its long-term outlook remains strong, justifying a "Hold" position.

title: Form 424B2 BARCLAYS BANK PLC
description: Barclays Bank PLC has filed a Form 424B2 with the SEC for $520,000 Autocallable Buffered Return Enhanced Notes due February 10, 2028. These notes are linked to an equally weighted basket of four equity securities: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc., Microsoft Corporation, Palo Alto Networks, Inc., and Snowflake Inc. The offering outlines key terms, risks, tax considerations, and details regarding potential payments at maturity or upon automatic call, emphasizing that the notes do not pay interest and involve principal loss risk.

title: Apple and Google pledge to improve app fairness
description: The UK Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is seeking views on commitments from Apple and Google to improve app store practices. These commitments aim to deliver greater certainty, transparency, and fairness for UK businesses dependent on app stores, boosting the UK's app economy and fintech sector. The proposals follow the designation of Apple and Google's mobile platforms as having strategic market status in October 2025, which subjects them to pro-competition rules.

title: Microsoft, CrowdStrike Lead 19 AI-Resilient Software Stocks
description: J.P. Morgan analysts have identified 19 software stocks, including Microsoft and CrowdStrike, that they believe are resilient to disruption from artificial intelligence technology. This comes after the software sector experienced a significant $2 trillion market value decline due to AI fears, which the analysts see as a buying opportunity. The report suggests these companies are well-positioned to navigate the evolving AI landscape.

title: Stoke Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ:STOK) Given Consensus Recommendation of "Moderate Buy" by Brokerages
description: Stoke Therapeutics (NASDAQ:STOK) has received a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating from eleven brokerages, with an average 12-month price target of $39.18. The company's stock opened at $31.99, boasts a market cap of $1.83 billion, and has seen notable institutional investment increases despite insider sales. Stoke Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing genetic medicines for rare neuromuscular and neurological disorders.

title: Leggett & Platt (LEG) Reports Earnings Tomorrow: What To Expect
description: Leggett & Platt (NYSE: LEG) is scheduled to report its Q4 earnings this Wednesday. Analysts anticipate a revenue decline of 11.1% year-on-year to $938.7 million and adjusted earnings of $0.23 per share. The company has missed Wall Street's revenue estimates in four of the last eight quarters, and analysts have largely reconfirmed their estimates, suggesting they expect the business to remain on its current trajectory.

title: NVDU: Providing Leverage To Nvidia, A Hot Tech Stock That Has Corrected (NASDAQ:NVDU)
description: Nvidia's rapid growth has led to its stock outperforming the NASDAQ and Apple, despite a high P/E ratio. The Direxion Daily NVDA Bull 2X Shares ETF (NVDU) amplifies NVDA's performance but carries significant risk due to its leveraged nature. A recent correction in NVDA's stock could present a buying opportunity, though its valuation remains high.

title: DeepSeek Panic Live Updates: Nvidia Stock Drops 4% As Trump Reportedly Targets China Exports
description: Nvidia's stock experienced a turbulent week, dropping 4% on Wednesday, amid concerns over a less capital-intensive AI model from China's DeepSeek and reports that Donald Trump is considering stricter export controls on semiconductor chip sales to China. The market volatility, driven by DeepSeek's competitive AI model and potential geopolitical shifts, prompted analysts to reassess the AI investment landscape, though many still view Nvidia's selloff as an overreaction. DeepSeek's emergence has led to significant shifts in market values for major tech companies and ignited debates about the future of AI development and U.S. competitiveness.

title: Major San Francisco Union Square hotel to hit the market as part of $1B sale
description: The Grand Hyatt San Francisco in Union Square is set to be put on the market by Host Hotels & Resorts Inc. as part of a larger $1 billion sale of West Coast properties. This divestment is consistent with Host's plan to offload "noncore" assets, including the 669-room hotel, which was acquired in a $1 billion three-hotel deal in 2018. The sale follows a recent strike by hotel workers and another significant change in San Francisco hotel ownership.

title: IUSV: A Cost-Effective Value ETF With Strong Downside Protection And Growth Potential
description: The iShares Core S&P U.S. Value ETF (IUSV) offers cost-efficient value investing with a portfolio of 700 U.S. large-cap and mid-cap value stocks and a low expense ratio of 0.04%. Its significant allocation to large-cap stocks and a 23% exposure to the technology sector, including Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon, contribute to strong downside protection and growth potential. IUSV has demonstrated superior performance, outperforming its peer IWD with a 167.2% total return over the past decade and 597.5% since inception.

title: Should Invesco Dividend Achievers ETF (PFM) Be on Your Investing Radar?
description: The Invesco Dividend Achievers ETF (PFM) is a passively managed large-cap value ETF with $719.98 million in assets, sponsored by Invesco. Launched in 2005, it focuses on dividend-paying companies that have increased dividends for over 10 consecutive years, with top holdings including Broadcom, Apple, and Microsoft. PFM has a Zacks ETF Rank of 3 (Hold) and an expense ratio of 0.52%, and its performance has shown year-to-date gains of 2.90% and a 19.34% increase over the last year as of January 22, 2025.

title: NYSEARCA:QYLD - High-Yield ETF with 12% Annual Income: Is It Still Worth It?
description: The NYSEARCA:QYLD ETF provides a high annual yield exceeding 12% through a covered call strategy on NASDAQ 100 stocks, making it attractive for income-focused investors. However, this strategy caps upside potential, leading to significant underperformance compared to broader market indices and newer, more flexible alternatives like JEPQ. While it offers consistent monthly income, its long-term capital preservation issues and inability to fully capitalize on bull markets raise questions about its suitability for total return investors.

title: Apple, Google And Meta Can't Escape Gambling Lawsuits, Judge Rejects Section 230 Shield In Landmark Ruling On Casino-Style Apps
description: A federal judge has ruled that Apple, Google, and Meta must face lawsuits alleging they profited from illegal casino-style apps, rejecting their Section 230 defense. The companies are accused of hosting and promoting addictive gambling apps and taking 30% commissions on in-app purchases, with plaintiffs seeking compensatory damages for alleged addiction and its consequences. This ruling adds to the growing legal scrutiny faced by tech giants.

title: Most of the ‘Sweet 16’ stocks have dominated. They might do it again in 2025.
description: The article discusses the "Sweet 16" stocks identified by Jefferies in September 2020, highlighting their significant contribution to the US stock market's performance over the past five years. It suggests these stocks, including Nvidia, may continue their dominant trend into 2025 due to expected revenue growth. The piece also mentions other dominant stock groupings like "BATMMAAN" and the "Magnificent Seven."

title: Most of the ‘Sweet 16’ stocks have dominated. They might do it again in 2025.
description: The article discusses the "Sweet 16" list of stocks, initially identified by Jefferies in September 2020, highlighting their significant contribution to the U.S. stock market's performance. It suggests that these stocks, including giants like Nvidia, are poised for continued revenue growth in 2025. The piece also mentions other groups of dominant stocks, such as "BATMMAAN," which includes Broadcom and the "Magnificent Seven."

title: EWC, Canada ETFs Rise Amid Trudeau Resignation
description: The iShares MSCI Canada ETF (EWC) rose following the announcement of Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's resignation, citing a need for new leadership. The article also discusses why Canadian ETFs have historically lagged behind U.S. equity ETFs like SPY, attributing it to factors such as sector concentration, innovation, market size, currency effects, venture capital, and global influence.

title: Schwab Trading Activity Index™: December Score Edges Higher for Third Consecutive Month
description: The Schwab Trading Activity Index™ (STAX) increased to 51.16 in December, marking its third consecutive monthly rise. Schwab clients net bought equities, adding exposure in Health Care and Energy sectors, while selling Information Technology and Communication Services. The report highlights market volatility, key economic data releases, and popular stocks bought and sold by Schwab clients throughout December and the entirety of 2024.

title: No Jitter Roll: Kore.ai Adds AI Agents, Intermedia Debuts Contact Center for Teams
description: This "No Jitter Roll" roundup details product news in enterprise communication. It highlights Kore.ai's new AI for Process platform with custom AI agents and Intermedia's Contact Center for Microsoft Teams. Other updates include Twilio's RCS features, RingCentral's Studio and AI Clips, and NUWAVE's Teleport migration product, alongside reports on AI agents, business service automation, and contact center technology dissatisfaction.

title: Intermedia intros contact centre for Microsoft Teams
description: Intermedia Cloud Communications has launched a new service that fully embeds unified communications (UC) and customer experience (CX) capabilities directly into Microsoft Teams, claiming it to be an industry first. This offering, Intermedia Contact Centre, enhances Teams with advanced contact centre functionalities like omnichannel support, enterprise-grade calling features, and AI functionality. The company, a Microsoft Gold Certified Partner, aims to transform Teams into a comprehensive platform for both UC and CX, elevating employee productivity and customer engagement.

title: FY25 Q2 - Press Releases - Investor Relations
description: Microsoft announced strong second-quarter fiscal year 2025 results, driven by significant growth in Microsoft Cloud and AI. The company reported $69.6 billion in revenue, a 12% increase, and a 17% increase in operating income to $31.7 billion. Microsoft's AI business alone surpassed an annual revenue run rate of $13 billion, marking a 175% year-over-year increase.

title: Nvidia calls China’s DeepSeek R1 model ‘an excellent AI advancement’
description: Nvidia praised China's DeepSeek R1 model as "an excellent AI advancement" despite concerns that it contributed to a 17% drop in Nvidia's stock. DeepSeek's open-source reasoning model reportedly outperformed leading U.S. models like OpenAI's, with significantly lower training costs. Nvidia views this breakthrough as a driver for more work for its GPUs, emphasizing "Test Time Scaling" as a new form of scaling law in AI development.

title: How Ciena keeps the internet online, with CEO Gary Smith
description: This article features an interview with Ciena CEO Gary Smith, discussing how the company's Wavelength Division Multiplexing (WDM) technology underpins the global internet by enabling massive data transmission over fiber optic cables. Smith details Ciena's evolution from serving telecom providers to becoming a critical partner for hyperscalers like Google and Meta, especially with the rise of AI. He also touches on the geopolitical considerations in network building and the company's long-term strategy for growth.

title: Aurora Mobile Partners With China's DeepSeek To Boost AI Capabilities
description: Aurora Mobile's enterprise AI platform, GPTBots.ai, has integrated China's DeepSeek R1 large language model (LLM), enhancing its capabilities alongside existing models like OpenAI, Meta Llama, and Google Gemini. This integration aims to provide businesses with more efficient, adaptable, and cost-effective advanced AI solutions, leveraging DeepSeek R1's strong reasoning tasks performance and affordability. The partnership positions GPTBots.ai as a comprehensive platform for diverse LLMs, allowing businesses to select models catering to specific operational, customer service, or marketing needs.

title: ETF of the Week: DGRW
description: This article highlights the WisdomTree US Quality Dividend Growth Fund (DGRW) as the ETF of the Week, discussed by VettaFi’s Head of Research Todd Rosenbluth and Chuck Jaffe. DGRW focuses on high-quality companies with a forward-looking approach to dividend growth, suitable as a core or complementary holding in a portfolio. Despite a modest 2% yield, it offers capital appreciation potential and has received strong ratings from Lipper and Morningstar.

title: Meta at ATH after Announcing Massive $65B CapEx Plan for AI Push 📈
description: Meta Platforms has announced a massive $60-65 billion capital expenditure plan for 2025, a significant increase aimed at bolstering its AI infrastructure and becoming a leader in the AI landscape. This aggressive investment includes building a new data center and acquiring 1.3 million GPUs, with the goal of developing Meta AI into a leading assistant serving over a billion users. Despite the substantial spending, market reaction has been largely positive, pushing Meta's stock to an all-time high as investors support the company's strategic AI focus.

title: Oracle surges, chip stocks rise as Trump unveils $500 bln AI plan
description: Oracle's shares jumped nearly 9% after former President Donald Trump announced a $500 billion AI infrastructure investment plan, "Stargate," involving Oracle, OpenAI, and SoftBank to keep the U.S. competitive in the global AI race. Other technology partners and AI-related companies, including Nvidia, Microsoft, Arm, Vistra, Dell Technologies, Super Micro Computer, and Palantir Technologies, also saw their stock prices increase following the news. The plan includes an immediate $100 billion investment, with the remaining over the next four years, and construction of data centers already underway in Texas.

title: Microsoft and OpenAI evolve partnership to drive the next phase of AI
description: Microsoft and OpenAI are continuing their strategic partnership, focusing on future AI developments and Stargate. The agreement, valid through 2030, maintains Microsoft's access to OpenAI's IP, revenue sharing, and exclusivity on OpenAI's APIs. Microsoft remains a major investor, while OpenAI has made a new, significant Azure commitment, and the exclusivity on new capacity has shifted to a right of first refusal for Microsoft.

title: CHAT: Generative AI Stocks Still Look Relatively Attractive
description: The Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT) has seen a significant rise of +13.60% since October 2024, outperforming the S&P 500. The ETF maintains a diversified portfolio with key holdings in NVIDIA, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Meta Platforms. While previously rated a "Strong Buy," the rating has been revised to "Buy" due to macroeconomic uncertainties, even as it appears between fair value and "slightly undervalued."

title: Board Games Market Set for Dynamic Boom as Key Players Hasbro • Mattel • Asmodee • Ravensburger
description: Coherent Market Insights Pvt. Ltd has released a new report titled "Board Games Market 2025-2032," providing an in-depth analysis of market trends, growth drivers, challenges, and opportunities. The report identifies key players such as Hasbro, Mattel, Asmodee, and Ravensburger, and segments the market by various board game types. It aims to help businesses forecast profitability and make strategic decisions by offering insights into market dynamics, regional outlook, and competitor landscapes.

title: Increased Fund Ownership Could Boost Microsoft’s Stock
description: Increased investment fund ownership in Microsoft could drive a bullish trend for its stock in 2025. Despite underperforming the S&P 500 in 2024, strong fund buying and anticipated positive earnings, fueled by AI investments, suggest potential gains. Analysts expect continued revenue growth and a favorable market environment for tech giants.

title: Qualcomm’s New Chips Will Bring AI to $600 Personal Computers
description: Qualcomm Inc. introduced its new Snapdragon X Platform chips at CES 2025, designed to power AI-capable personal computers priced as low as $600. These chips feature an 8-core Oryon central processor, a graphics component, and a dedicated AI chip, and will run Microsoft Corp.'s Copilot+ software. Major PC manufacturers like Dell Technologies Inc. and Lenovo Group Ltd. are expected to release laptops using this new technology in early 2025.

title: Intel Extends Leadership in AI PCs and Edge Computing at CES 2025
description: At CES 2025, Intel unveiled new Intel Core Ultra (Series 2) processors, including the 200V series with Intel vPro for businesses, and 200HX and H series for creators and gamers, all featuring cutting-edge AI enhancements and performance improvements. Intel also introduced a new suite of processors for edge computing, showcasing significant performance gains in AI workloads compared to previous generations and competitors. The availability of these new systems is set to begin in January and February 2025, with further releases planned for the first half of the year.

title: Tech Stocks Are Still Pricey. Dip Buyers Should Look Here Instead.
description: Investors seeking "buy-the-dip" opportunities might be advised to look beyond tech stocks, which remain expensive despite recent market fluctuations. Instead, the article suggests considering other sectors, highlighting that the Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund has seen a significant drop from its recent high. This implies that industrial stocks may present a more attractive dip-buying opportunity than pricey tech stocks.

title: AMZY ETF: No Longer An Attractive Entry Point (Rating Downgrade) (NYSEARCA:AMZY)
description: The YieldMax AMZN Option Income Strategy ETF (AMZY) has delivered significant returns primarily through dividends since its last "Buy" rating, but its price has remained largely unchanged. While the ETF's strategy effectively passes underlying equity gains to investors via dividends, current market conditions and Amazon's valuation are stretched, making it a less attractive entry point. This reassessment suggests caution and a potential shift in investment strategy despite the fund's past performance.

title: FY25 Q2 - Press Releases - Investor Relations
description: Microsoft announced strong second-quarter fiscal year 2025 results, driven by significant growth in Microsoft Cloud and AI. The company reported $69.6 billion in revenue, a 12% increase, and a 17% increase in operating income to $31.7 billion. Microsoft's AI business alone surpassed an annual revenue run rate of $13 billion, marking a 175% year-over-year increase.

title: How Ciena keeps the internet online, with CEO Gary Smith
description: This article features an interview with Ciena CEO Gary Smith, discussing how the company's Wavelength Division Multiplexing (WDM) technology underpins the global internet by enabling massive data transmission over fiber optic cables. Smith details Ciena's evolution from serving telecom providers to becoming a critical partner for hyperscalers like Google and Meta, especially with the rise of AI. He also touches on the geopolitical considerations in network building and the company's long-term strategy for growth.

title: Mytheresa to Rename Parent Company After YNAP Acquisition
description: Mytheresa plans to rename its parent company, MYT Netherlands Parent B.V., to LuxExperience after it finalizes the acquisition of Yoox-Net-a-Porter (YNAP). This rebranding signifies Mytheresa's ambition to become a major luxury e-commerce player, aiming for a combined company revenue of €4 billion by 2029. The move follows Mytheresa's successful growth strategy amid a luxury slowdown by focusing on top-spending clients.

title: Twin Cities Pride dumps Target Corp. as sponsor over DEI rollback
description: Twin Cities Pride has ended its nearly two-decade sponsorship with Target Corp. after the retailer rolled back its diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) policies. An online fundraiser quickly raised over $50,000 to offset the lost funding, exceeding the expected donation from Target. Minnesota Senator Scott Dibble criticized Target's decision as a "total capitulation" to former President Trump's "politics of cruelty."

title: Walmart Boosts Regional Manager Pay As Retailers Struggle To Find Leaders
description: Walmart is significantly increasing bonus and stock awards for regional store managers, with top performers potentially earning over $600,000, in response to difficulties retailers face in filling managerial roles. This move comes as the company also reduces perks for office staff, including phasing out remote work and restructuring health insurance plans. The article details the pay increases for market managers, contrasting it with tech giant layoffs and Walmart's recent financial performance.

title: IUSV: A Cost-Effective Value ETF With Strong Downside Protection And Growth Potential
description: The iShares Core S&P U.S. Value ETF (IUSV) offers cost-efficient value investing with a portfolio of 700 U.S. large-cap and mid-cap value stocks and a low expense ratio of 0.04%. Its significant allocation to large-cap stocks and a 23% exposure to the technology sector, including Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon, contribute to strong downside protection and growth potential. IUSV has demonstrated superior performance, outperforming its peer IWD with a 167.2% total return over the past decade and 597.5% since inception.

title: 3 Fidelity ETFs You Can Buy and Hold Forever to Generate $100,000 in Yearly Dividend Income, Starting in 2025
description: This article examines three Fidelity ETFs that could generate significant dividend income. It details the Fidelity International Value Factor ETF (FIVA), the Fidelity International High Dividend ETF (FIDI), and the Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF (FYEE), outlining their dividend yields, expense ratios, holdings, and potential risks. The piece calculates the approximate investment needed in each ETF to achieve $100,000 in annual dividend income.

title: Milka chocolate sold in Canada recalled for containing undeclared nuts
description: Milka chocolate bars, specifically the Bubbly Alpine Milk product, have been recalled across Canada due to undeclared hazelnuts, posing a risk to those with allergies. The Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) issued the recall, noting the product was distributed in several provinces and sold online. Mondelez International, owner of Milka, stated the product was not authorized for sale in Canada by their Canadian entity and attributed the issue to an incorrect label applied by an unauthorized distributor.

title: JPMorgan Chase reportedly joins the 5-days-a-week RTO wave
description: J.P. Morgan Chase & Co. is reportedly joining Goldman Sachs in requiring employees to work from the office five days a week, hinting at a potential new norm for financial services firms. CEO Jamie Dimon has been a strong proponent of in-person work, stating that "In general, there's nothing like face-to-face." This move comes despite warnings from some experts about potential employee dissatisfaction and signaling employer mistrust regarding all-or-nothing work arrangements.

title: Schwab Trading Activity Index™: December Score Edges Higher for Third Consecutive Month
description: The Schwab Trading Activity Index™ (STAX) increased to 51.16 in December, marking its third consecutive monthly rise. Schwab clients net bought equities, adding exposure in Health Care and Energy sectors, while selling Information Technology and Communication Services. The report highlights market volatility, key economic data releases, and popular stocks bought and sold by Schwab clients throughout December and the entirety of 2024.

title: Tech Stocks Are Still Pricey. Dip Buyers Should Look Here Instead.
description: Investors seeking "buy-the-dip" opportunities might be advised to look beyond tech stocks, which remain expensive despite recent market fluctuations. Instead, the article suggests considering other sectors, highlighting that the Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund has seen a significant drop from its recent high. This implies that industrial stocks may present a more attractive dip-buying opportunity than pricey tech stocks.

title: Prologis CEO Hamid Moghadam to retire next year
description: Hamid Moghadam, co-founder and CEO of Prologis, plans to retire on January 1, 2026, after more than four decades with the company and its predecessor. Dan Letter, current president, will succeed him as CEO, while Moghadam will transition to executive chairman. This succession plan has been in the works for over two years, with Letter having joined AMB in 2004 and becoming president of Prologis in January 2023.

title: Adeia Announces Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Results
description: Adeia announced strong financial results for Q4 and Full Year 2024, achieving record post-separation revenue and cash from operations and signing 10 deals in Q4, including new strategic agreements with Amazon, Canon, and a luxury retailer. The company also paid down $50 million in debt and repurchased $20 million of common stock, while growing its patent portfolio by 12% to over 12,000 assets. Adeia provided a positive financial outlook for full year 2025, projecting revenue between $390.0 million and $430.0 million.

title: Best Buy offering free online higher education to all employees
description: Best Buy is expanding its partnership with Strayer University to offer free, no-debt online higher education options to all full- and part-time U.S. employees from their first day of employment. This Degrees@Work program provides opportunities to earn a certificate, associate, bachelor's, or master's degree, with coursework paid directly by Best Buy. This initiative aligns with other major retailers like Amazon, Walmart, and Target, which also provide significant education benefits to their employees.

title: UPS: A Cheap Stock to Buy After Bad News That Really Wasn’t
description: Despite a recent selloff triggered by announced cuts in Amazon shipping, Morningstar believes UPS stock is undervalued, offering long-term upside potential. The company's strong competitive advantages, including its cost efficiency and scale, are expected to support margin improvement, especially as it reduces its lower-margin Amazon business. UPS is highlighted as a "wide-moat" stock with attractive metrics for investors.

title: Carver Bancorp Inc. (CARV) reports earnings
description: Carver Bancorp Inc. (CARV) has reported a net loss of $5.6 million for the quarter ended December 31, 2024, a significant decrease from a net income of $19 thousand in the prior year.
