This loss is attributed to reduced net interest income, lower non-interest income, and increased non-interest expenses. The company also saw a decrease in total assets and an increase in nonaccrual loans, though its capital ratios remain above regulatory requirements.

title: Earnings call transcript: Martin Marietta Q4 2024 beats EPS forecast, stock dips
description: Martin Marietta (NYSE: MLM) reported Q4 2024 earnings per share of $4.79, beating the forecast of $4.64, though revenue slightly missed expectations at $1.63 billion. Despite the earnings beat, the stock slipped 3.31% in pre-market trading. The company provided a positive outlook for 2025, projecting 4% growth in aggregate shipments and a 6.5% increase in pricing, driven by infrastructure demand and data center construction.

title: 3 Top Value Stocks to Buy and Hold for 2025
description: Morningstar highlights three value stocks—Campbell, Danaher, and UPS—to buy and hold for 2025, selected from their "Best Companies to Own" list. These companies possess wide economic moats, predictable cash flows, and are currently undervalued. The article provides a brief overview of each company's investment thesis and Morningstar's fair value estimates.

title: 3 More Stocks to Buy Before They Rebound
description: This article from Morningstar discusses the current market environment, including tariff impacts and upcoming economic reports, and features an interview with Morningstar's Chief US Market Strategist, Dave Sekera. Sekera provides updates on several companies, including Alphabet, Amazon, Advanced Micro Devices, and Palantir, after their recent earnings reports, and recommends three stocks to buy: Alphabet (GOOGL), SLB Ltd (SLB), and United Parcel Service Inc Class B (UPS). He also touches on Gilead Sciences, Kraft Heinz, Papa John's, Eli Lilly, Novo Nordisk, Estée Lauder, and FMC.

title: Apollo Commercial Real Estate Finance Inc (ARI) reports earnings
description: Apollo Commercial Real Estate Finance Inc (ARI) reported its 2024 fiscal year earnings, showing a decrease in net interest income to $198.98 million and a net loss of $131.9 million for common stockholders. The company detailed its $6.7 billion portfolio in commercial mortgage loans and highlighted a significant increase in its Specific CECL Allowance and a realized loss from the extinguishment of the Massachusetts Healthcare Loan. Apollo also outlined its financing strategy, including secured debt arrangements and dividends declared to shareholders.

title: News | Cousins Properties buys into office rebound with $1 billion investment spree
description: Cousins Properties invested nearly $1 billion in office properties during the fourth quarter of 2024, acquiring three trophy assets across the Sun Belt, including the Sail Tower in Austin and Vantage South End in Charlotte. This investment spree signals the company's belief in an office market rebound, positioning itself to meet growing demand for high-quality office space. The company's strategy focuses on well-located, amenitized spaces, which has contributed to an 89.2% occupancy rate and significant leasing activity in 2024.

title: BlackRock, Inc. Increases Stake in Tyler Technologies Inc
description: BlackRock, Inc. increased its stake in Tyler Technologies Inc by acquiring an additional 713,164 shares, bringing its total holdings to over 4 million shares. This transaction, valued at $576.64 per share, reflects BlackRock's continued interest in the software industry and Tyler Technologies' strong growth potential, despite its current overvaluation according to GuruFocus. Other notable investors also hold positions in Tyler Technologies, highlighting broader market confidence in the government software solutions provider.

title: 5 Charts on How Investors Are Addressing Race at Shareholder Meetings
description: Investor engagement on race and ethnicity at shareholder meetings has evolved, with a significant increase in anti-DEI resolutions but consistently low support for them. While overall support for conventional resolutions addressing race has declined, there's a growing focus on workforce-related issues, aligning with broader human capital management trends. Requests for pay gap reports and anti-harassment efforts at major companies like Apple, Amazon, Nike, Oracle, and Tesla garnered significant shareholder backing in 2024.

title: Target is sued for defrauding shareholders about DEI
description: Target is facing a proposed class-action lawsuit for allegedly misleading shareholders about the risks of its diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) initiatives. Shareholders claim the company's focus on social goals led to massive financial losses and a plummeting stock price due to consumer backlash. The lawsuit seeks damages for shareholders who purchased Target stock between August 2022 and November 2024.

title: Should Invesco Large Cap Growth ETF (PWB) Be on Your Investing Radar?
description: The Invesco Large Cap Growth ETF (PWB) offers broad exposure to the Large Cap Growth segment of the US equity market, managing over $1.03 billion in assets with an expense ratio of 0.53%. It primarily allocates to Information Technology and its top holdings include Tesla, Alphabet, and Amazon. PWB has a Zacks ETF Rank of 2 (Buy), making it a suitable option for investors seeking exposure to large-cap growth.

title: BlackRock, Inc. Increases Stake in Compass Therapeutics Inc
description: BlackRock, Inc. significantly increased its stake in Compass Therapeutics Inc by acquiring an additional 67,163 shares, bringing its total holdings to 6,887,615 shares. This move solidifies Compass Therapeutics as 5.00% of BlackRock's portfolio, reflecting the investment firm's strategic interest in the biopharmaceutical sector. Despite a GF Score of 43/100, indicating potential challenges, Compass Therapeutics' stock has seen a remarkable 115.17% gain since the transaction, driven by its innovative therapeutic pipeline.

title: Technology in 2025: The Cycle Rolls On
description: Goldman Sachs Asset Management foresees a robust technology sector in 2025, driven by AI momentum and a broadening of opportunities beyond the "Magnificent 7." The article highlights investment potential in hyperscalers, software companies benefiting from AI, and ASIC semiconductor manufacturers, emphasizing the importance of active management to identify growth areas.

title: Technology in 2025: The Cycle Rolls On
description: Goldman Sachs Asset Management foresees a broadening tech cycle in 2025 beyond the "Magnificent 7," driven by AI advancements. They anticipate significant investment opportunities in hyperscalers, software companies benefiting from AI, and ASIC semiconductor firms, emphasizing the role of active management to identify growth and manage risk. The article highlights sustained tech spending trends and optimism for ROI among hyperscalers despite large capital expenditures.
